header.title: Basic Info
notes: This report includes the most recent 219 arXiv papers related to the keyword "(all:"ptychography" OR all:"electron microscopy")".

items.title: Determining Electron Beam Lateral Coherence in a Scanning Electron Microscope Using Electron Diffraction
items.summary: This study develops STEM capabilities within an SEM to measure electron beam lateral coherence. By analyzing interference patterns from Au flakes and graphene at 30 keV, the authors extract a lateral coherence lower limit of approximately 60% over 5% of the beam diameter. These findings confirm the system's potential for quantum-coherent electron-light-matter interaction experiments.

items.title: Vibrational high-harmonics and period-doubling bifurcation probed by time-resolved electron diffraction
items.summary: This research introduces event-based convergent beam electron diffraction to map the spatio-temporal dynamics of nanoscale mechanical resonators. Probing an optically driven silicon membrane, the authors successfully capture nonlinear phenomena including Duffing behavior and period-doubling bifurcations. The approach achieves 5 ns temporal resolution and nanometer spatial resolution, offering a route toward probing nonlinear nanomechanics at the atomic scale.

items.title: High temperature transitions in Ruddlesden-Popper nickelates La$_{n+1}$Ni$_{n}$O$_{3n+1}$
items.summary: Investigates the complete series of bulk-stable Ruddlesden-Popper nickelates La$_{n+1}$Ni$_n$O$_{3n+1}$ using a comprehensive suite of experimental techniques from 2 to 1000 K. The study identifies a previously underappreciated high-temperature phase transition distinct from the known transition to a tetragonal phase, clarifying the structural properties of these superconducting-related materials.

items.title: PtyRANNOSAUR: Ptychography with Robust Artificial Neural Networks Optimized for Sub-Angstrom Accuracy and Ultrafast Reconstruction
items.summary: The authors present PtyRANNOSAUR, a neural network framework for ultrafast reconstruction of atomic-resolution electron ptychography data. By mapping 4D-STEM data to 2D phase images, the system achieves sub-0.5 Å resolution 10-100x faster than standard iterative methods. The model robustly handles spatial coherence and multiple scattering, enabling near-live, state-of-the-art imaging across diverse material systems.

items.title: Orientation Mapping via Dictionary Indexing of AC-STEM Kikuchi patterns using Open-source Software
items.summary: This study develops an open-source template-matching approach for orientation mapping using aberration-corrected STEM transmission Kikuchi diffraction. By employing dynamical simulations and geometric indexing, the authors demonstrate the method on fuel-cell and battery materials. The results show that simulated patterns align closely with experimental data, providing high orientation sensitivity and spatial resolution for nanoscale characterization of polycrystalline materials.

items.title: Valence-change-driven reduction of antiphase boundaries in spinel ferrite epitaxial films
items.summary: This research investigates the effect of post-oxidation annealing on antiphase boundaries (APBs) in spinel ferrite epitaxial films. The study finds that post-oxidation significantly reduces APB density through a topotactic solid-state reaction where Fe ions are oxidized. This process increases antiphase domain size and enhances magnetic properties, such as saturation magnetization, while preserving the film's crystal structure and epitaxial strain.

items.title: Quantum tomography of free electrons
items.summary: The authors present a universal approach for quantum-state tomography of free electrons in continuous variables. By using interfering quantum paths from spectrally shifted laser waves, the method reveals the density matrix and essential properties of electron wavepackets. The study demonstrates how many-body Coulomb interactions modify a single electron's state, providing a tool for optimizing quantum states in free-electron optics and microscopy.

items.title: Hydrofluoric acid-free titanium etching for rare-event searches
items.summary: This study investigates sulphuric acid as a safer, hydrofluoric acid-free alternative for etching titanium in rare-event search experiments. Results show that 40% sulphuric acid at 40°C effectively removes surface material (up to 3.5 mg/cm²), providing a viable method for reducing radiopurity-limiting surface contamination in sensitive underground laboratory environments.

items.title: One-dimensional self-organization of water molecules in proton conducting Andersson-Wadsley titanates
items.summary: This research explores the hydration and proton conductivity of Andersson-Wadsley titanates. It finds that water molecules self-organize into one-dimensional double chains along the b-axis, facilitating highly anisotropic superionic conductivity of 3 mS/cm at room temperature. This suggests that these ordered water chains enable fast proton transport through a Grotthuss-like mechanism.

items.title: Operando spectro-ptychography reveals dynamical charge-storage and degradation pathways in redox-active electrodes
items.summary: This work develops an operando soft X-ray spectro-ptychography platform to track chemical and morphological changes in redox-active electrodes. Applied to an alkaline Fe anode, the study identifies that reversible charge storage via rapid hydroxide insertion competes with a slower dissolution-redeposition process that causes degradation. This approach enables the separation of fast charge-storage and slow degradation pathways at the nanoscale.

items.title: Anomalous charge density wave in a two-dimensional superatomic superconductor
items.summary: This study investigates charge density wave (CDW) behaviors in the 2D superatomic superconductor Au6Te12Se8. The authors identify an anomalous CDW governed by transverse phonons with a massive 4 Å displacement. Findings reveal that the CDW transition temperature increases significantly as the material approaches the superatomic limit, where it competes with superconductivity, offering new insights into electron-phonon interactions.

items.title: Accurate identification and measurement of the precipitate area by two-stage deep neural networks in novel chromium-based alloys
items.summary: This paper introduces DT-SegNet, a two-stage deep learning framework combining YOLOv5 and SegFormer, to automate precipitate measurement in chromium-based superalloys. The objective is to improve upon manual microstructural analysis. Results show that DT-SegNet significantly outperforms existing tools like Weka and ilastik in segmentation accuracy, providing a robust solution for high-throughput alloy development and microstructural characterization.

items.title: Room-Temperature Noncolinear Ferroelectricity in van der Waals WO$_2$Cl$_2$ with a Wide Bandgap
items.summary: This study demonstrates room-temperature ferroelectricity in the van der Waals material WO2Cl2. Researchers found that the material possesses a wide 2.80 eV bandgap and a large dipole. Crucially, they directly observed an exotic noncollinear dipole order at the atomic level, proving that WO2Cl2 overcomes common weaknesses in low-dimensional ferroelectrics, such as small bandgaps or weak polarization.

items.title: Persistent structural distortions and absent superconductivity in trilayer nickelate thin films
items.summary: This research investigates the absence of strain-induced superconductivity in trilayer (n=3) La4Ni3O10 nickelate thin films. While compressive strain suppresses density waves, superconductivity remains absent. The authors identify persistent, layer-inequivalent octahedral rotations around the c-axis as a unique structural distortion that likely inhibits superconductivity, suggesting that epitaxial strain alone is insufficient for inducing superconductivity in this specific system.

items.title: Controllable Growth and Characterization of α- and β-phase MnSe by Chemical Vapor Deposition
items.summary: This study demonstrates the controllable growth of phase-pure α- and β-phase MnSe on sapphire using chemical vapor deposition. Key findings show that precursor vapor pressure controls flake size and β-phase films exhibit antiferromagnetism with a Néel temperature of 53 K. These results establish MnSe as a tunable platform for 2D spintronic and optoelectronic applications.

items.title: Coordination-Sensitive Nanoscale Analysis of Defect-Driven Phase Transformation in Si-Doped (AlXGa1-X)2O3
items.summary: This study introduces a coordination-sensitive atom probe tomography framework to quantify local cation coordination and link it to defect-driven phase transformations in Si-doped (AlxGa1-x)2O3. It reveals that gamma-phase inclusions emerge under high Al content and heavy Si doping, characterized by significant coordination loss. DFT results confirm that Al incorporation and donor-induced vacancies facilitate this coordination collapse and phase instability.

items.title: Hydration-controlled twist forms a moiré glass in charge-frustrated layered silicates
items.summary: This research investigates the natural formation of moiré superlattices in montmorillonite clay. The authors discovered that hydration-controlled processes create a 'moiré glass' state with preferred low-angle twists stabilized by lattice-charge disorder. The study establishes dynamic hydration as a macroscopic handle for programming twist in layered materials, where dehydration arrests specific configurations in discrete steps.

items.title: Transverse Modulation of Continuous Electron Beams by a Structured Optical Cavity
items.summary: This study proposes a phase plate for continuous electron beams using ponderomotive interaction within a Fabry-Pérot resonator to achieve compact aberration correction. The researchers demonstrate that a Laguerre-Gaussian mode can fully compensate for third-order spherical aberration in a 5 keV beam, reducing the focal probe width to 1.4 Å.

items.title: Dislocation-templated antiferromagnetic domains in epitaxial NiO
items.summary: This study investigates the control of antiferromagnetic domains in epitaxial NiO/MgO(001) using electron channeling contrast imaging. It finds that interface dislocation networks deterministically pin twin-domain walls, creating a robust structural memory effect during thermal cycling. The results demonstrate that antiferromagnetic textures can be engineered by manipulating the density and spatial configuration of one-dimensional misfit dislocations.

items.title: Sub-8-nm resolution AKB-mirror-based hard X-ray ptychography via generalized Wirtinger projections
items.summary: This study aims to achieve high-resolution hard X-ray ptychography using achromatic AKB mirror optics. By developing the Generalized Wirtinger Projections (GWP) algorithm, the researchers achieved sub-8-nm resolution. GWP improves spatial resolution and partial-coherence reconstruction while enhancing GPU memory efficiency, facilitating advanced 3D spectroscopic imaging at the nanoscale.

items.title: Tracking atomic-scale interdiffusion in immiscible bimetallic nanoparticles via four-dimensional electron tomography
items.summary: This study uses 4D atomic resolution electron tomography and in situ STEM to track interdiffusion in immiscible PdIr nanoparticles. It reveals a temperature-driven miscible transition pathway involving surface reconstruction at 200°C, Ir interfacial diffusion at 400°C, and collective inward diffusion at 900°C. These findings provide quantitative atomic-scale insights into how metastable nanostructures evolve into thermodynamically stable configurations at the nanoscale.

items.title: Extending the La Solubility Limit in Sr$_3$Ir$_2$O$_7$ through High-Pressure High-Temperature Synthesis
items.summary: This research aims to extend the La solubility limit in bilayer iridates using high-pressure high-temperature synthesis. The authors successfully increased La incorporation up to x=0.23, observing a transition from a localized magnetic insulating state to metal-like electronic behavior. These findings demonstrate that high-pressure synthesis reveals electronic and magnetic states inaccessible through conventional ambient-pressure methods.

items.title: Low-Dose 3D Bonding Mapping Through "Soft" Core-Loss EELS Tomography and Unsupervised Deep Learning
items.summary: This study aims to achieve dose-efficient 3D bonding mapping of beam-sensitive nanomaterials using STEM-EELS. By combining shallow Fe-M2,3 edges with an unsupervised deep learning method (DIPm-TV), the researchers achieved 1 nm isotropic resolution and 50x higher dose efficiency, successfully resolving the core-shell structure and internal voids of FeO/Fe3O4 nanocubes.

items.title: Extending Field Limits in Nanoscale Magnetic Imaging with Metamaterial-inspired Magnetic Flux Concentrators
items.summary: The paper aims to extend magnetic field limits in nanoscale imaging using sample-integrated magnetic flux concentrators (MFCs). Experiments demonstrate that MFCs locally amplify magnetic fields by a factor of five, enabling the observation of magnetization reversal and domain evolution in magnetite nanoparticles and magnetofossils that were previously inaccessible due to instrumental constraints.

items.title: Unsupervised Deep Learning for Limited-Angle STEM-EDX Tomography -- Application to 3D Chemical Analysis of Phase-Change Memory Devices
items.summary: This research develops an unsupervised deep learning framework, DIPm-TV, to improve 3D chemical mapping in limited-angle STEM-EDX tomography. The method successfully compensates for severe missing-wedge artifacts, enabling near-isotropic 3D elemental reconstruction of Ge-Sb-Te memory devices without requiring external structural priors, even under low-dose conditions.

items.title: Improved selector behavior in ultrathin chromium-doped V$_2$O$_3$ films
items.summary: This study investigates the electrical and structural properties of ultrathin chromium-doped V2O3 films for use as selector elements. Findings show that switching effects persist down to 5 nm thickness with improved leakage and transition abruptness. TEM analysis attributes these improvements to a thin amorphous interface layer and titanium diffusion from the electrodes.

items.title: Design and electron optics performance of a MEMS electrostatic electron monochromator
items.summary: The authors design a compact, MEMS-fabricated electrostatic electron monochromator to improve energy resolution in electron microscopy. Simulations show the design achieves 19 meV resolution while maintaining a 128 pA beam current. This fringe-field-based approach offers a cost-effective alternative to complex state-of-the-art monochromators, potentially bringing high-resolution energy filtering to standard scanning electron microscopes.

items.title: Context-Aware Deep Learning for Defect Classification in Atomic-Resolution STEM
items.summary: This work introduces a context-aware deep learning framework to improve defect classification in atomic-resolution STEM. By integrating image contrast with experimental metadata like composition and beam energy, the model achieves over 98% accuracy on simulations. This approach transforms ambiguous image-only tasks into physically grounded problems, significantly reducing classification entropy and matching human-level performance on experimental data.

items.title: Unconventional incommensurate epitaxy of superconducting FeSe films on SrTiO$_3$
items.summary: This study examines the epitaxial relationship between superconducting FeSe films and SrTiO3 substrates using XRD and STEM. The researchers discovered an unconventional growth regime where FeSe maintains directional alignment with the substrate but lacks atomic registry, retaining its own lattice spacing without forming misfit dislocations. This suggests that crystallographic orientation can be preserved without atomic matching at the interface.

items.title: High-resolution TEM imaging on 3D nanocrystals with tilt illuminations
items.summary: This paper proposes a method to obtain high-resolution projected images of 3D nanocrystals by mitigating contrast transfer function (CTF) mixing in TEM. By employing tilted illumination and a ring slit filter, the technique produces images comparable to STEM. This approach is particularly useful for imaging complex structures like MOFs and perovskites where small molecules or ions are critical.

items.title: Ptychographic Algorithms for Phase Recovery in 4D Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy
items.summary: This thesis examines ptychographic algorithms for phase recovery in 4D STEM, specifically ePIE, WDD, and SSB. It successfully implements an SSB reconstruction script on simulated MoS2 data and records experimental 4D STEM data. The results provide a mathematical framework for these methods and demonstrate the practical application of SSB for specimen imaging.

items.title: Unravelling the Role of Stacking Disorder on the Optoelectronic Properties of Zn3P2
items.summary: This study uses electron microscopy and DFT to investigate planar defects in Zn3P2 thin films. It identifies defects arising from Zn sublattice vacancy ordering with very low formation energy. While these defects do not introduce mid-gap states and are electronically benign, they likely degrade performance by acting as preferential segregation sites for optically active point defects.

items.title: Crystallizing Substrates Drag Supported Nanoparticles
items.summary: This research investigates how substrate crystallization influences the migration of supported nanoparticles using in situ TEM and 4D-STEM. The study finds that advancing amorphous-to-crystalline fronts in AlOx films actively drag Pt nanoparticles over long distances. Phase-field simulations confirm that this deterministic transport is driven by asymmetric interfacial energy contrast across the moving boundary.

items.title: Optical Signature of Moiré Superlattices Formed by Twisted SrTiO$_3$ Membranes
items.summary: This study investigates the structural and optical properties of millimeter-scale moiré superlattices formed by twisted SrTiO3 membranes at a 36-degree angle. The researchers discovered new low-frequency Raman-active vibrational modes and strong second harmonic generation at the interface, indicating an asymmetric, twisted interface. These findings demonstrate enhanced interlayer coupling and provide a method for tuning phononic and nonlinear optical properties in twisted oxides.

items.title: X-ray Fractional Orbital Angular Momentum from Coherent Magnetic Scattering
items.summary: Investigates if odd-charge topological defects in artificial spin ice generate fractional orbital angular momentum in X-ray beams. The study confirms antiferromagnetic ground states with superdomain walls and observes integer OAM at structural peaks alongside fractional OAM at magnetic peaks. In thermally active samples, the fractional OAM beam exhibits dynamic rotation due to phase discontinuity fluctuations.

items.title: Reversible Superdense Ordering of Tetragonal Lithium in a Layered Material
items.summary: Employs multimodal scanning transmission electron microscopy to map lithium intercalation and dynamics in layered LaTe3. The study identifies three ordered phases and discovers a reversible, superdense three-layer lithium phase with tetragonal symmetry occupying the van der Waals gap at high concentrations. This approach provides a complete tracking of lithium ordering, which is crucial for advancing next-generation energy storage applications.

items.title: Hydrothermally-Assisted Sintering of Calcium Hydroxide Sputtering Targets: A Route to Quantum-Grade CaO Thin Films
items.summary: Demonstrates the production of high-purity calcium hydroxide sputtering targets using hydrothermally-assisted sintering at low temperatures. The resulting targets achieve 98% theoretical density while preserving the brucite phase. These targets were successfully used to deposit (002) oriented epitaxial calcium oxide thin films on sapphire substrates, providing a scalable route to quantum-grade materials for thin-film applications.

items.title: Impact of Cu-Mn ratio on Structure and Defects in Layered Multiferroic Cu1-xMn1+ySiTe3
items.summary: Investigates the influence of Cu-to-Mn ratios and nanoscale defects on the properties of multiferroic Cu1-xMn1+ySiTe3. Scanning transmission electron microscopy reveals that Cu-deficient crystals exhibit extensive stacking faults and chemical inhomogeneity, whereas Cu-rich crystals show fewer stacking faults but contain needle-shaped precipitates. Calculations confirm the Cu-rich structure is more polar, offering guidance for designing multiferroic materials with tailored performance.

items.title: Sharp periodic Ge concentration modulations beyond the conduction band valley wavevector $k_0$ in nuclear spin-free Si quantum wells
items.summary: Aims to enhance conduction-band valley splitting in Si quantum wells through periodic Ge modulations. Using molecular beam epitaxy, the study demonstrates laterally homogeneous modulations with periods down to 0.49 nm. Simulations suggest that steep trapezoidal 2k0/3 heterostructures significantly enhance valley splitting, whereas k0-type modulations show a much weaker effect, providing a route for scalable spin-qubit development.

items.title: Magnetic domains reconfiguration on the Fe3O4(110) surface across the Verwey transition by Spin-Polarized Low-Energy Electron Microscopy
items.summary: Uses spin-polarized low-energy electron microscopy to map the vector magnetization of the Fe3O4(110) surface across the Verwey transition. At room temperature, magnetic domains align with bulk <111> easy axes. Below the transition, magnetization reconfigures toward in-plane [100] and [001] directions, reflecting monoclinic symmetry changes. The findings confirm that magnetization remains within the surface plane throughout the transition.

items.title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
items.summary: Develops an interpretable vision-transformer workflow to automate the classification of fracture causes in alumina matrix composite implants. The model achieves high accuracy by identifying diagnostic signals like mirror geometry and hackle lines. Crucially, performance at low magnification (50x) is comparable to high-magnification inspection, suggesting that macro-scale features provide sufficient data for reliable quality assurance and pre-screening.

items.title: Spatiotemporal Electron Microscopy of Phonon Polaritons in MoO3
items.summary: Investigates the spatiotemporal dynamics of phonon polaritons in anisotropic α-MoO3 by extending the bandwidth of photon-induced near-field electron microscopy deeper into the infrared. The study visualizes the spatial distribution and wavelength-dependent lifetimes of polaritons within cavity-like flakes. These advancements demonstrate the potential of PINEM for probing the complex polaritonic properties of van der Waals materials in hard-to-access spectral ranges.

items.title: Self-Assembly of Lipid-Biopolymer Periodic Nanostructures on Photonic Length Scales
items.summary: Investigates how lipids and biopolymers co-assemble to produce structural color in insects. By using lipid vesicles as templates for biopolymers, the study demonstrates the self-assembly of periodic nanostructures with periodicities up to 1.2 micrometers. These results provide a methodology for recreating the physicochemical mechanisms underlying biophotonic structural colour and overcoming periodicity limits in synthetic lipid-based systems.

items.title: Synthesis and properties of bulk Mg$_3$WN$_4$ in a wurtzite-derived structure
items.summary: Reports the first bulk synthesis of Mg3WN4 in a wurtzite-derived crystal structure via solid-state metathesis. In situ X-ray diffraction tracked the reaction progress, while second harmonic generation measurements confirmed the material's polar symmetry. The study reveals that bulk wurtzite Mg3WN4 is prone to defect formation and demonstrates that carefully controlling the thermal budget allows for the selective synthesis of phase-pure ternary nitrides.

items.title: Multiscale Vectorial Determination of Magnetic Order Parameters using Electron Magnetic Linear Dichroism
items.summary: Demonstrates electron magnetic linear dichroism as a quantitative tool for mapping vectorial magnetic order parameters with nanometer resolution. By combining EELS with dynamical diffraction simulations, the study reconstructs the magnetic spin axis in antiferromagnetic and ferromagnetic FeRh. The method is robust at the atomic scale and effectively separates magnetic signals from nonmagnetic anisotropy, providing a new pathway for imaging altermagnets and antiferromagnets.

items.title: Electron-beam induced methane decomposition for in-situ carbon doping of hexagonal boron nitride
items.summary: Presents a method for nanoscale carbon doping of hexagonal boron nitride using electron-beam irradiation in a methane atmosphere. The beam simultaneously generates vacancies and decomposes methane, leading to progressive carbon incorporation into the lattice. EELS mapping shows that carbon forms hexagonal patches approximately 1 nm in size, primarily confined to the irradiated area, which modifies the local electronic environment for defect engineering.

items.title: Magneto-Optical Detection of Anisotropic Spin Currents in Altermagnetic RuO2
items.summary: Investigates charge-to-spin conversion in 12-nm-thick altermagnetic RuO2 films using optical probes. The study detects directionally anisotropic spin polarization that aligns with altermagnetic symmetry and is strongly suppressed along specific crystallographic directions. Scanning transmission electron microscopy reveals persistent strain in the films, which may support the observed magnetic behavior, establishing RuO2 as an efficient spin source for field-free spintronic devices.

items.title: Mid-infrared Fourier ptychographic upconversion imaging
items.summary: This study implements a high-resolution mid-infrared upconversion imaging system using elliptical pumping and Fourier ptychography to overcome aperture restrictions. By combining synthetic aperture and phase retrieval, it achieves a 39 μm resolution and a space-bandwidth product tenfold larger than previous benchmarks. The system demonstrates single-photon sensitivity, enabling high-throughput infrared imaging for industrial and biomedical applications.

items.title: Atomic-Scale Observation of Symmetry Breaking in Altermagnetic MnTe
items.summary: Provides direct atomic-scale evidence of coexisting polar distortions and altermagnetic order in MnTe using STEM and electron magnetic chiral dichroism. The study reveals that MnTe is not a uniform g-wave altermagnet but hosts inversion-symmetry-breaking distortions. These distortions lower the symmetry to admit d-wave and s-wave spin splitting, suggesting that local lattice symmetry can be used to control altermagnetic properties.

items.title: kikuchipy: an open-source toolbox for analysis of EBSD patterns
items.summary: Introduces kikuchipy, an open-source Python toolbox for analyzing electron backscatter diffraction patterns. The software supports Hough and dictionary indexing, orientation refinement, and result validation. It demonstrates effective phase differentiation and orientation analysis in various alloys, providing a flexible, community-driven resource for advanced electron microscopy workflows and improving indexing results through fast iteration.

items.title: Local Structural Signatures of Shear Bands in Metallic Glasses via Electron Nanodiffraction
items.summary: Investigates structural changes in metallic glass shear bands using electron nanodiffraction and cryogenic ion polishing. The study identifies nanoscale, stripe-like regions oriented at 45 degrees to applied strain. These regions exhibit reduced centrosymmetry and increased shear and normal strain, demonstrating that plastic deformation in metallic glasses is driven by coordinated nanoscale structural transformations.

items.title: Three-Dimensional Atomic-Scale Structural Transformation in a SrTiO3 Grain Boundary
items.summary: Employs multislice electron ptychography to resolve the three-dimensional atomic structure of a SrTiO3 grain boundary. The study reveals a transition from symmetric to asymmetric configurations hidden in 2D projections. Findings link these 3D structures to distinct local chemical environments, vacancy distributions, and oxygen octahedral rotations, highlighting the importance of depth-resolved characterization for engineering complex oxide functionalities.

items.title: Resilience of the physicochemical properties of graphene-based materials for applications in harsh radiation environments
items.summary: This research investigates the effects of 60 MeV 35Cl ion irradiation on graphite (HOPG) and multilayer reduced graphene oxide (ML-rGO) for use in harsh environments. Findings reveal that while HOPG suffers from reduced crystallinity and electrical performance, ML-rGO undergoes partial structural reorganization and forms more ordered sp2 domains. This suggests that initial material order significantly influences radiation tolerance in carbon-based devices.

items.title: Processing-Controlled Structural Uniformity and Oxide-Ion Conduction in Na0.52Bi0.47TiO3 Ceramics Probed by Eu3+ Photoluminescence
items.summary: Evaluates how precursor mixing methods influence the structural uniformity and oxide-ion conduction of Na0.52Bi0.47TiO3 ceramics using Eu3+ photoluminescence. The study finds a processing-driven trade-off between bulk defect generation and grain-boundary blocking. Dry-ground samples exhibit higher bulk conductivity, while wet-ground samples show lower grain-boundary resistance, with Ca-doping further improving conductivity to 18.98 mS cm-1 at 600 °C.

items.title: Photoluminescence Identification of Multiple Local Eu3+ Environments in BaTiO3 Ceramics
items.summary: Utilizes Eu3+ photoluminescence as a sensitive probe to identify multiple local environments in BaTiO3 ceramics sintered at various temperatures. While conventional diffraction shows a standard tetragonal phase, photoluminescence spectra and decay analysis reveal two distinct Eu-related local configurations. The results demonstrate that optical probing can track local structural heterogeneity and processing-dependent changes inaccessible to average lattice techniques.

items.title: Cathodoluminescence Analysis of Defects and Grain Boundaries in Zn3P2 Thin Films Grown on Graphene by MOVPE and MBE
items.summary: This study investigates defects and grain boundaries in Zn3P2 thin films grown on graphene using MOVPE and MBE. The researchers linked antiphase boundaries to optical emission modulation and found that grain boundaries act as non-radiative sinks for charge carriers. MOVPE-grown films showed larger grains and improved material quality compared to MBE, highlighting grain-size control as essential for optimizing photovoltaic absorbers.

items.title: A new method to probe conducting filaments in MoS$_2$-based memristors
items.summary: The authors develop a novel mechanical exfoliation technique to investigate conducting filaments in MoS2-based memristors. By selectively removing the top electrode, they used KPFM and Raman mapping to reveal that filament formation occurs through metallic atom migration into the MoS2 layer. The study demonstrates that the choice of metallic electrode significantly influences switching behavior due to differences in adsorption and diffusion energies.

items.title: A Local Probe Mass Spectrometer for Localized and Sensitive Product Detection in Environmental Electron Microscopy
items.summary: This research develops a Local Probe Mass Spectrometer (LPMS) integrated with environmental transmission electron microscopy to correlate atomic-scale structural changes with reaction products. By sampling gas locally via a micro-capillary, the system overcomes dilution issues in open-cell setups. Validation using Co3O4 nanoplates demonstrates simultaneous detection of reaction products and atomic-scale dynamics, establishing a framework for quantitative structure-reactivity correlation in catalysis.

items.title: Electron modulation and ultrafast near-field imaging with vectorial laser fields
items.summary: This study explores the direct coupling of laser light with electron beams using vectorial laser fields. The researchers demonstrate that longitudinally polarized light can modulate electron beams coherently and linearly without nanostructured materials. Furthermore, vectorial polarizations were used to image three-dimensional nanophotonic near-fields in metallic mesocrystals, enabling the tilt-free generation of attosecond electron pulses and novel imaging modes for metamaterial tomography.

items.title: Stacking-order-dependent electronic properties of MoTe2/WSe2 moiré bilayers
items.summary: This study calibrates optical second harmonic generation for MoTe2/WSe2 moiré bilayers using scanning transmission electron microscopy to evaluate stacking-order-dependent properties. By comparing transport and magnetic data across distinct stacking orders, the authors clarify the nature of the Chern insulator and the mechanisms of electric-field-tuned metal-insulator transitions, establishing a consistent relationship between stacking order and the electronic phase diagram.

items.title: Oxygen-Pressure-Limited Recovery of the Hematite α-Fe$_2$O$_3$(0001) Surface from a Reduced Fe$_3$O$_4$(111)-Like Layer
items.summary: Using real-time microscopy, this study investigates the oxidation kinetics of hematite surfaces from a reduced magnetite-like layer. It finds that complete surface recovery is linked to the nucleation and lateral growth of a two-dimensional honeycomb phase. Crucially, the oxidation rate is limited by oxygen availability, with growth slowing significantly below a specific oxygen partial pressure threshold, highlighting the role of oxygen supply.

items.title: Multiferroic Properties of Electrospun CFO-BCTSn Nanocomposites for Magnetoelectric and Magnetic Field Sensing Applications
items.summary: This research synthesizes lead-free CFO-BCTSn multiferroic composite nanofibers using sol-gel electrospinning to evaluate their potential for magnetoelectric sensing. Characterization confirms the coexistence of spinel and perovskite phases. The nanofibers exhibit both magnetic and piezoelectric properties, with magnetoelectric coupling evidenced by differences in magnetic hysteresis loops between electrically poled and unpoled samples, supporting their application in nanoscale magnetoelectric devices.

items.title: Gold Bipyramids as a Promising Alternative to Gold Nanorods for Analytical and Biomedical Applications
items.summary: This study compares gold bipyramids and gold nanorods for analytical and biomedical applications. Findings show that bipyramids offer significantly higher spectral quality factors, a threefold increase in SERS enhancement, and greater sensitivity to refractive index changes compared to nanorods. Furthermore, bipyramids demonstrate high efficiency as thermosensitizers for photothermal therapy, as evidenced by the effective photothermal killing of E. coli under laser irradiation.

items.title: Enhancement in Magnetic and Magnetocaloric Properties of CoFe2O4 Nanofibers at Lower Temperatures
items.summary: This paper investigates the magnetic and magnetocaloric properties of CoFe2O4 nanofibers synthesized via sol-gel electrospinning. The nanofibers exhibited a pure cubic spinel structure and ferromagnetic behavior, with a saturation magnetization of 75.87 emu/g. Significant magnetocaloric effects were observed at low temperatures, including a maximum magnetic entropy change of 1.71 J/K at 32 K, indicating potential for low-temperature electronic applications.

items.title: Quantifying the coupling between strain and cation valence in high entropy oxide thin films using electron microscopy
items.summary: This study employs advanced S/TEM techniques to quantify the relationship between nanoscale strain and cobalt valence in high entropy oxide thin films. The researchers found that substrate temperature significantly influences local structural modulations and defect states, even in films with identical nominal compositions. These findings establish a framework for tailoring the functional properties of high entropy oxides through precise control of synthesis conditions.

items.title: Photolithography-Only Fabrication of Transmons Using Double-Oblique Evaporation
items.summary: This study investigates a photolithography-only fabrication process for transmon Josephson junctions using double-oblique evaporation. The researchers successfully fabricated functional transmon devices with a T1 of approximately 9 microseconds. These results demonstrate the feasibility of realizing functional superconducting qubits without electron-beam lithography, simplifying the production of transmon junctions while maintaining relevant performance metrics.

items.title: Nanoscale Thermal Imaging of Dislocation-Mediated Heat Transport
items.summary: This research maps nanoscale temperature distributions across individual dislocations in SrTiO3 using in situ electron microscopy. The study reveals a 47 K temperature drop across a dislocation array, identifying core-dominated and inter-core heat transport effects. These findings quantitatively resolve the spatial heterogeneity of dislocation-mediated thermal resistance, providing a microscopic basis for defect engineering in functional materials.

items.title: Prebiotic magnetite enables chirality-magnetic surface feedback
items.summary: This study explores the magnetic properties of magnetite synthesized under prebiotic conditions to understand its role in biomolecular homochirality. Researchers found that prebiotic magnetite particles exhibit single-vortex and multi-vortex states. Simulations demonstrate that these grains undergo irreversible re-magnetization when interacting with homochiral compounds, suggesting a robust mechanism for storing and reinforcing chiral bias on the early Earth.

items.title: An evaluation framework for sparse 4D (3D + time) imaging reconstruction via bootstrapped cross-validation
items.summary: This paper presents a bootstrapped cross-validation framework to evaluate the performance of sparse 4D imaging reconstructions in the absence of a ground truth reference. By quantifying correlations between reconstructions from independently sampled data subsets, the method enables qualitative and quantitative assessment. Validated on simulated X-ray datasets, this approach provides a reference-free strategy for optimizing ultrafast imaging experiments.

items.title: Microstructure evolution during rapid solidification of hypoeutectic Al-Ag alloys near absolute stability
items.summary: Microsegregation-free microstructures can form by solidifying at velocities beyond the absolute stability limit ($V_{\text{abs}}$), where solute partitioning is suppressed by a stable, planar solid-liquid interface. Producing such microstru

items.title: Crystal growth and characterization of the ultra-high temperature substrate $\mathrm{Ta_{1-x}Hf_{x}C_{0.5}}$
items.summary: Incorporation of $\mathrm{Al_{y}Ga_{1-y}N}$ (AGN) semiconductors into high power electronics offers efficiency improvements in power transmission, generation, and use, if approaches to eliminate the defects arising from film-lattice mismatc

items.title: Compositional and Magnetic Characterisation of Oblique Co and Fe Nanowire Structures Fabricated Using Focused Electron Beam Induced Deposition
items.summary: This study investigates the elemental composition and magnetic induction of 3D Co and Fe nanowires fabricated via FEBID at varying growth angles. It finds that metal content decreases as the oblique growth angle increases. Optimizing electron beam parameters—specifically using lower voltage and higher current—mitigates this reduction, maintaining uniform metal content for growth angles up to 60°.

items.title: Magnetocaloric Effect in Nanostructured $La_{0.6}Sr_{0.4}Fe_{1-x}Co_{x}O_3$
items.summary: This research examines the magnetocaloric effect in nanostructured La0.6Sr0.4Fe1-xCoxO3 perovskites. It finds that substituting iron with cobalt enhances ferromagnetic coupling, increasing saturation magnetization and the Curie temperature. The cobalt-rich sample achieved the highest entropy change, demonstrating that combining cobalt doping with controlled nanostructuring effectively optimizes the material's magnetocaloric response.

items.title: High-temperature behavior of amorphous alumina coatings: Insights from in-situ nanoindentation and X-ray diffraction studies
items.summary: This study evaluates the high-temperature mechanical properties and structural stability of amorphous Al2O3 coatings. Findings show that hardness decreases with temperature while the Young's modulus remains constant, reflecting increased plasticity. In-situ X-ray diffraction revealed crystallization begins at 700°C, with the material transitioning to a stable alpha-Al2O3 phase above 950°C, defining the coating's thermal application limits.

items.title: Topological and morphological signatures of disorder in a self-assembled, soft matter sponge network
items.summary: This paper analyzes topological and morphological differences between ordered double-gyroid and disordered sponge networks in block copolymers. It reveals the sponge network is primarily trivalent but lacks the intercatenated loops of the double-gyroid. This suggests the sponge is a disordered single-gyroid variant, offering a topological method to distinguish and map the boundary between ordered and disordered phases.

items.title: Characterizing Dislocation Substructures in Creep-Deformed Olivine Using Electron Channeling Contrast Imaging
items.summary: This study applies electron channeling contrast imaging (ECCI) to characterize dislocation substructures in creep-deformed olivine. It finds that ECCI reliably reveals complex subgrain boundaries and dislocation loops, providing essential constraints for modeling dislocation creep in Earth's mantle minerals.

items.title: Correction of STEM Distortions
items.summary: This paper derives mathematical transformations to correct scanning distortions in Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy (STEM) images. These transformations provide a technical foundation for correction algorithms implemented in the CEOS Panta Rhei and TEMDM software packages, enabling more accurate image reconstruction.

items.title: Indirect Detection of Lactate Through Voltammetry Using Glassy Carbon Microelectrodes
items.summary: The study aims to achieve indirect voltammetric detection of non-electroactive lactate using glassy carbon microelectrodes. By immobilizing lactate oxidase in a chitosan matrix, the researchers successfully detected lactate at concentrations as low as 10 nM through enzymatic hydrogen peroxide generation, demonstrating a robust sensing platform.

items.title: Moire based strain analysis in wurtzite GaAs -- rock-salt (Pb,Sn)Te core-shell nanowires grown by molecular beam epitaxy
items.summary: This study investigates core/shell GaAs/(Pb,Sn)Te nanowires grown by molecular beam epitaxy to estimate strain in the topological crystalline insulator shells. Using high-resolution TEM and geometric phase analysis, the authors identify misfit dislocations and moiré fringes, demonstrating that moiré pattern analysis serves as an effective alternative for quantifying strain in core-shell nanowire structures.

items.title: Emergence of a correlated insulating state in bulk 1T-NbSe$_2$ via metal intercalation
items.summary: Researchers synthesized bulk 1T-NbSe2 using electrochemical Sn intercalation, a phase previously limited to monolayers. While DFT predicts metallic behavior, the intercalated samples exhibit an insulating state driven by electronic correlations. This work establishes electrochemical intercalation as a viable method to stabilize inaccessible bulk polytypes and provides a new platform for studying correlated electronic states.

items.title: Local droplet etching-assisted quantum dot epitaxy for telecom C-band quantum light emitters
items.summary: The study proposes local droplet etching (LDE) to fabricate symmetric, low-density InGaAs quantum dots for telecom C-band applications. Key findings include narrow photoluminescence emission lines and confirmed single-photon emission with low g(2)(0) values. The results demonstrate that LDE is a versatile and scalable approach for creating high-quality quantum light sources with tailored emission properties.

items.title: Moire based strain analysis in wurtzite GaAs -- rock-salt (Pb,Sn)Te core-shell nanowires grown by molecular beam epitaxy
items.summary: This research investigates strain in GaAs/(Pb,Sn)Te core-shell nanowires using transmission electron microscopy. The study finds that moiré patterns and misfit dislocations at the interface serve as an effective alternative for estimating strain in crystalline topological insulator shells, facilitating the characterization of lattice mismatch.

items.title: Characterization of Photopolymerized Microscopic Chiral Structures Using Photonic Orbital Angular Momentum
items.summary: Demonstrates a low-cost platform for fabricating microscale chiral structures using maskless photolithography and characterizing them via helical dichroism with orbital angular momentum (OAM) vortex beams. The study achieved helical dichroism signals of ~30%, establishing OAM as a robust, spatially selective chiral probe that eliminates the need for expensive femtosecond sources or specialized plasmonic substrates.

items.title: A Two-Step Chemical Vapor Deposition Process for the Synthesis of an Ir(111)/Borophene/2D-Hexagonal Boron Nitride Heterostructure by Intrinsic Segregation
items.summary: Reports a two-step chemical vapor deposition process to synthesize a vertical Ir(111)/borophene/hexagonal boron nitride heterostructure. By exploiting boron's finite solubility in iridium and intrinsic segregation during cooling, the researchers formed a borophene monolayer beneath a boron nitride overlayer. This approach offers a scalable route for producing high-quality, large-area vertical heterostructures of two-dimensional materials.

items.title: Emergence of Localized Surface Plasmons in Unpatterned Hyperdoped Polycrystalline Silicon
items.summary: Demonstrates that localized surface plasmon resonances can emerge in unpatterned hyperdoped polycrystalline silicon films without external nanostructuring. The study finds that mid-infrared plasmonic responses originate from metal-dielectric interfaces at grain boundaries. This universal phenomenon allows for cost-effective, microelectronics-compatible plasmonic devices by utilizing the natural grain structure of doped semiconductors to tune light-matter interactions.

items.title: Structural evolution of Ti/Cu multilayers as a function of period thickness
items.summary: This study examines how period thickness affects the morphology and crystallization of Ti/Cu multilayers. It finds that period thickness dictates the balance between interfacial intermixing and crystallization. Smaller periods exhibit reduced periodicity and intermixing, while larger periods show more regular structures with progressive crystallization and texture development, particularly in fcc Cu(111) and Ti layers.

items.title: Oxygen Vacancies at Dislocation Core Modulate Plasticity in Strontium Titanate
items.summary: Investigates the correlation between dislocation core structure, oxygen vacancy density, and mobility in SrTiO3. Using STEM and MD simulations, the study finds that oxygen vacancy evolution at the core depends on gliding distance and is intrinsically coupled with plasticity, suggesting a mechanism where vacancies modulate dislocation mobility in oxides.

items.title: TrueEBSD in MTEX: automatic image matching for correlative microscopy applications
items.summary: Introduces TrueEBSD, an open-source MTEX add-on for automatic image alignment and spatial distortion correction in correlative microscopy. By augmenting EBSD maps with SEM data, the tool enables quantitative crystallographic measurements, such as phase fraction and grain boundary susceptibility, which are unattainable when analyzing datasets separately.

items.title: An Advanced Epitaxial Strategy Enabling Vertical GaN Devices on Silicon Wafers
items.summary: Presents a sputtering-based strategy to grow high-quality GaN epitaxial films on silicon with low vertical resistance. By forming a sub-nanometer silicide-based template, the researchers created an amorphous-like interlayer that relaxes strain and accommodates lattice mismatch, enabling device-grade vertical performance and ohmic behavior for cost-effective power electronics and micro-LEDs.

items.title: Fast reduction of electron-beam-activated graphene oxide by an infrared laser pulse
items.summary: This research investigates the rapid reduction of graphene oxide using a synergistic electron-beam-assisted near-infrared laser pulse. The study finds that electron beam irradiation creates defects that enhance NIR absorption and oxygen diffusion, enabling a 90% reduction of a 46-nm film within 960 ns. This process locally restores sp2 bonding while introducing turbostatic disorder.

items.title: Polar Topologies in a Ferroelastic Metal Membrane
items.summary: This study investigates polar textures in freestanding SrRuO3 membranes. By releasing epitaxial films from substrates, researchers observed ferroelastic domain refinement that generates emergent polar topologies. Key findings reveal electric polarization selectively at translation-inequivalent antiphase boundaries through Neel-like tilt interpolation and within 90-degree ferroelastic walls due to strain mismatch and rotostriction, demonstrating that metal oxide membranes can host nanoscale polar textures.

items.title: Statistical analysis of virion-cell interactions mediated by peptide nanofibrils and peptide amphiphiles using STEM tomography
items.summary: The paper introduces a STEM tomography framework to statistically analyze how peptide nanofibrils and amphiphiles govern virion-cell interactions. It reveals that while all tested peptides efficiently capture virions, they employ distinct spatial organization strategies that likely determine their viral transduction-enhancing efficacy.

items.title: Tracking visible pulsed laser annealing of Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 heterostructures with in situ transmission electron microscopy
items.summary: This study uses in situ transmission electron microscopy to monitor HZO heterostructure crystallization under visible nanosecond laser pulses. It identifies a sharp energy threshold, finding that a single 177 mJ/cm2 pulse yields 86% of the ferroelectric orthorhombic phase. Simulations show substrate melting limits temperatures to 1900 °C, supporting a kinetic crystallization pathway via the tetragonal phase.

items.title: Beyond conventional skyrmions in synthetic antiferromagnets
items.summary: Researchers investigate skyrmion formation in synthetic antiferromagnets using asymmetric CoB and CoFeB sublattices. They identify two distinct skyrmion families—conventional and inverse polarity—governed by the competition between external magnetic fields and interlayer RKKY exchange. Findings demonstrate that these textures reside exclusively in the CoFeB layers, providing a method for engineering programmable 3D spin textures for skyrmion-based computing.

items.title: Circular Phase Representation and Geometry-Aware Optimization for Ptychographic Image Reconstruction
items.summary: This paper presents a deep learning framework for ptychographic reconstruction that models phase on a unit circle using cosine and sine components. By employing a differentiable geodesic loss, the method avoids branch-cut discontinuities and wrapping artifacts. Results demonstrate superior reconstruction, better preservation of high-frequency content, and significant speed improvements over traditional iterative solvers while maintaining physical consistency.

items.title: Tracking visible pulsed laser annealing of Hf$_{0.5}$Zr$_{0.5}$O$_2$ heterostructures with in situ transmission electron microscopy
items.summary: This study tracks the visible pulsed laser annealing of Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 (HZO) heterostructures using in situ transmission electron microscopy. The researchers identified a sharp laser energy threshold for crystallization, finding that a single 177 mJ/cm² pulse produces an 86% ferroelectric orthorhombic phase.

items.title: Visualizing Crystallization Dynamics and Transformation Pathways of Disordered Rocksalt Oxides During Thermally Activated Sol-Gel Synthesis
items.summary: This study investigates the crystallization dynamics of disordered rocksalt Li1.2Mn0.4Ti0.4O2 during sol-gel synthesis. Using multiscale characterization, it identifies two pathways: a multi-step transition via stable intermediates and a kinetic shortcut where intermediates dissolve into an amorphous matrix to directly precipitate the target structure. Macroscopically, transformation primarily proceeds through spinel and lithium titanite intermediates.

items.title: Quantum echo-enabled high harmonic generation using ultrafast electrons
items.summary: The study introduces a quantum echo-enabled high-harmonic generation (QEEHG) scheme to produce tunable, coherent radiation by manipulating electron wavepacket phases. By combining multiphoton PINEM scattering with dispersive chirps, the approach induces quantum interference to selectively enhance high-harmonic orders while suppressing unwanted radiation. This method establishes a new paradigm for compact coherent light sources and quantum wavefunction shaping in ultrafast electron microscopy.

items.title: Mechanical Scaling Laws and Deformation Behavior of Nanoporous Tantalum Microparticles
items.summary: This study investigates the mechanical scaling laws of nanoporous tantalum produced via liquid metal dealloying. It finds that stiffness and hardness scale with solid volume fraction according to Gibson-Ashby predictions, unlike nanoporous gold. This behavior is attributed to enhanced ligament connectivity enabled by solvent chemistry, with molecular dynamics simulations confirming dislocation-dominated plasticity during indentation.

items.title: Influence of Ni Doping on the Structural, Morphological, Optical, and Electrical Properties of Nanocrystalline Cd1-xMnxS Thin Films
items.summary: Researchers investigated the effects of Ni doping on Cd1-xMnxS thin films for optoelectronic applications. They found that increasing Ni concentration improves crystallinity, increases grain size, and reduces the optical band gap from 2.72 to 2.62 eV. The doped films exhibit high optical transmittance and enhanced photoconductivity, demonstrating their potential as efficient window layers for thin-film solar cells.

items.title: Accurate Nanoscale Mapping of Electric Fields across Random Grain Boundaries in Polycrystalline Oxides Using Precession-Assisted 4D-STEM
items.summary: This study develops a precession-assisted 4D-STEM method to accurately map electric fields across grain boundaries in polycrystalline oxides. By combining electron beam precession with advanced post-processing, the researchers achieved unbiased diffraction shift measurements. This approach successfully extracts local electric fields and charge distributions in BaTiO3 and SrTiO3, overcoming the limitations of conventional center-of-mass analysis and dynamical scattering.

items.title: Electric-Field Control of Quantum Tunneling Regimes in Focused He-Ion-Beam-Irradiated Oxide Interfaces
items.summary: This research explores using helium focused ion beam irradiation to create gate-tunable potential barriers at oxide interfaces. The study finds that irradiation-induced lattice deformation enables the fabrication of tunnel field-effect transistors. Electrostatic backgating allows continuous tuning of the barrier profile, enabling controlled access to thermionic emission, direct tunneling, and Fowler-Nordheim tunneling regimes within a single device architecture.

items.title: Modulation of Spin Angular Momentum of Emission in Symmetric 1D Plasmonic Crystals by Cathodoluminescence
items.summary: This study investigates circularly polarized light (CPL) generation in symmetric 1D plasmonic crystals using electron beam excitation. It demonstrates that coherent interference between transition radiation and plasmonic modes produces energy- and momentum-resolved CPL. The findings reveal that CPL efficiency can be modulated by the excitation position, offering a platform for nanoscale spin angular momentum control without structural chirality.

items.title: Predictive drift compensation of multi-frame STEM via live scan modification
items.summary: This paper presents a method to mitigate sample drift in STEM by analyzing past frames to predict and adjust future sampling-grid points. By offsetting the scan-grid framewise and pixelwise, the technique removes long-range drift and minimizes intra-image warping. The framework is effective for atomic-resolution and in-situ video across various scan patterns.

items.title: Imaging the transverse component of optical near-fields in resonant photonic structures
items.summary: This study uses ultrafast 4D scanning transmission electron microscopy to image optical near-fields in resonant photonic structures. By visualizing the transverse Lorentz force in silicon nanostructures, the authors demonstrate that these structures enable efficient transverse electron streaking at optical frequencies. The measured spatial profiles match numerical simulations, validating the use of U4DSTEM for high-resolution near-field mapping.

items.title: Drift Correction of Scan Images by Snapshot Referencing
items.summary: Introduces snapshot-referencing (SSR) drift correction to eliminate spatial distortions in scanning electron microscopy. By using a fast-scan snapshot as a reference and modeling drift with Bezier and piece-wise linear functions, the method restores spatial integrity in hyperspectral data cubes. This software-based solution is applicable to various probe-based analytical techniques without requiring specialized hardware.

items.title: Proximity Magnetism in Mn(Bi,Sb)2Te4-(Bi,Sb)2Te3/MnTe Natural Heterostructures
items.summary: Investigates magnetic and topological orders in self-organized Mn(Bi,Sb)2Te4-(Bi,Sb)2Te3/MnTe natural heterostructures. The study finds that Mn(Bi,Sb)2Te4 mediates exchange fields, resulting in an anomalous Hall effect with an enhanced interfacial Neel temperature exceeding 200 K. This architecture enables robust, field-free spin-orbit torque switching at low current densities, demonstrating significant potential for practical spintronic applications.

items.title: Organosilane-functionalized hydrothermal-derived coatings on titanium alloys for hydrophobization and corrosion protection
items.summary: This research examines the impact of NaOH hydrothermal treatment and hexadecyltrimethoxysilane (HDTMS) coating on Ti-6Al-4V alloy properties. While alkaline treatment initially reduces corrosion resistance, subsequent HDTMS functionalization achieves significant hydrophobicity (147° contact angle) and improves corrosion protection in NaCl solutions. The results suggest this dual treatment is ideal for enhancing titanium alloy durability in moisture-exposed applications.

items.title: Crystallographic Challenges in Microscopy of Multidomain Spinel Materials
items.summary: Evaluates STEM-HAADF imaging and Fourier filtering for characterizing delta-DRX spinel domains. Simulations show that interfaces between eight variants produce four Fourier filtered profiles, one of which is undetectable along the [110] axis. The results suggest that apparent disorder may be slanted domain boundaries, highlighting the need for careful interpretation of atomic-resolution micrographs in multi-domain materials.

items.title: Laser induced surface nitriding of niobium: phase evolution and superconducting behaviour
items.summary: This study investigates laser-induced nitriding of niobium to evaluate phase evolution and superconducting properties. It establishes a processing map for beta and gamma phases, finding that gamma-phase formation increases the critical temperature to approximately 15 K. Additionally, a uniform beta-layer quadruples surface microhardness, providing a method to engineer mechanically robust and superconducting Nb-N layers.

items.title: Direct Orientation Contrast Imaging of Anti-Phase Domains on III-V Materials Using Scanning Electron Microscopy
items.summary: This research examines direct orientation contrast imaging of zinc-blende III-V materials using scanning electron microscopy. By analyzing GaP on GaAs, the study quantifies anti-phase domain contrast relative to electron beam energy and tilt angles. Findings show that image processing reveals in-plane preferential anti-phase boundaries for GaP on Si, facilitating the characterization of III-V materials grown on non-polar substrates.

items.title: Anion Ordering and Phase Stability Govern Optical Band Gaps in BaZr(S,Se)3
items.summary: This study investigates mixing thermodynamics and phase stability in the BaZr(S,Se)3 system using simulations and electron microscopy. It identifies a layered anion-ordered structure at 33% sulfur and maps the temperature-composition phase diagram. Findings reveal that composition, structure, and anion ordering control the optical band gap, with selenium alloying and ordering enabling tuning between 1.6 and 1.9 eV.

items.title: SPARSE -- Efficient High-Resolution SEM Imaging of Rare Microstructural Features Across Large Areas by Selective Rescanning
items.summary: The authors developed SPARSE, an open-source Python framework for efficient high-resolution SEM imaging of rare microstructural features. By using a two-stage selective rescanning approach, the framework achieved a 95% detection rate in dual-phase steel while reducing acquisition time to just 19% of conventional methods.

items.title: Damage dose dependence of deuterium retention in high-temperature self-ion irradiated tungsten
items.summary: This study investigates deuterium (D) retention in tungsten irradiated by self-ions at 1350 K. It finds that D concentration increases with damage dose without saturation, reaching 1.7 at.% at 2.3 dpa. High-temperature irradiation induces nm-sized voids where D is trapped as both gas and surface atoms, a mechanism distinct from lower-temperature irradiation behaviors.

items.title: Tuning Structure and Magnetism in Large-Scale 2D Ferromagnet Fe$_3$GeTe$_2$ through Ni Doping
items.summary: This research uses molecular beam epitaxy to control Ni-doping in 2D ferromagnetic Fe3GeTe2 films. Findings show that Ni substitution and intercalation shrink lattice parameters, suppress perpendicular magnetic anisotropy, and drastically reduce the Curie temperature to 50 K. Theoretical calculations confirm these effects are due to altered magnetic exchange interactions and magnetocrystalline anisotropy energies.

items.title: Depth-Resolved Thermal Conductivity of HFCVD Diamond Films via Square-Pulsed Thermometry
items.summary: This study measures the depth-resolved thermal conductivity of HFCVD diamond films on SiC using square-pulsed source thermometry. It finds that thermal conductivity increases from 60 W/m·K at the nucleation interface to 200 W/m·K at the surface. This gradient directly reflects microstructural grain coarsening, offering a physically grounded model for heat transport in diamond-based thermal management layers.

items.title: Tuning Structure and Magnetism in Large-Scale 2D Ferromagnet Fe$_3$GeTe$_2$ through Ni Doping
items.summary: This research investigates the effects of Ni doping on the structural and magnetic properties of epitaxial Fe3GeTe2 films grown via molecular beam epitaxy. The study finds that Ni substitution and intercalation lead to lattice shrinkage, a suppression of perpendicular magnetic anisotropy, and a drastic reduction in the Curie temperature.

items.title: On the origin of superlattice stacking faults nucleation via climb of Frank partial in CoNi-based superalloys
items.summary: Investigates superlattice stacking fault (SF) nucleation in CoNi-based superalloys at 850°C, demonstrating that non-conservative climb of Frank partials is a viable pathway for forming both intrinsic and extrinsic SFs. Key findings include the first experimental confirmation of negative climb-assisted extrinsic fault nucleation and the role of solute segregation in reducing fault energy to enable sustained climb.

items.title: Favorable half-Heusler structure of synthesized TiCoSb alloy: a theoretical and experimental study
items.summary: Identifies the most favorable crystal structure of synthesized TiCoSb half-Heusler alloy for thermoelectric applications using Rietveld refinement, electron microscopy, and first-principles calculations. The study determines the most stable structure among four candidates by corroborating theoretical energy estimations with experimental data, establishing the crystallized lattice structure and providing a foundation for evaluating its thermoelectric properties.

items.title: Decoding Superconductivity in La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_{7-δ}$ Thin Films via Ozone-Driven Structure and Oxidation Tuning
items.summary: This study investigates structural and electronic prerequisites for superconductivity in epitaxial La3Ni2O7-δ thin films. Using STEM and EELS to correlate stacking polymorphs with transport behavior, the researchers find that homogeneity in oxygen stoichiometry, epitaxial strain, and structural motifs are critical for stabilizing the superconducting phase. These findings offer a framework for designing ambient-pressure superconducting nickelates.

items.title: Ferromagnetic interlayer exchange coupling in a few layers of CrSBr on a gold thin film
items.summary: This research uses spin-polarized low energy electron microscopy to image the magnetic texture of thin CrSBr on gold. It discovers a ferromagnetic ground state for thicknesses under 11 nm, stabilized by electron transfer from the gold substrate. This demonstrates that substrate engineering can effectively tune the magnetic properties and electronic band structure of two-dimensional magnets for spintronic applications.

items.title: Toward Neutrino and Dark Matter Detection with Ancient Minerals: TEM Study of Heavy-Ion Tracks in Olivine
items.summary: This study evaluates olivine as a 'paleo-detector' for neutrinos and dark matter by analyzing heavy-ion tracks using STEM. The researchers found that ion irradiation produces robust, measurable tracks at the MeV scale, with continuity changes reflecting the transition between electronic and nuclear stopping powers.

items.title: Physics-Informed 3D Atomic Reconstruction and Dynamics of Free-Standing Graphene from Single Low-Dose TEM Images
items.summary: This framework reconstructs 3D atomic coordinates of free-standing graphene from single low-dose TEM images with sub-angstrom accuracy. It identifies a critical dose threshold for structural recovery and demonstrates that millisecond-scale electronic modulations are driven by sub-angstrom structural fluctuations. The findings link local geometry and strain to electron localization, providing insights for beam-sensitive 2D materials.

items.title: Photo-Assisted Pd-Nb2O5/Carbon Nanocomposites for Enhanced Ethanol Electro-Oxidation Kinetics and CO Tolerance in Alkaline Media
items.summary: Researchers developed Pd-Nb2O5/C nanocomposites to improve ethanol electro-oxidation and CO tolerance in alkaline media. The optimized catalyst reduced onset potential by 160 mV and increased poisoning tolerance fivefold. Light irradiation further doubled current density and enhanced stability, showing that synergistic oxide-metal interactions and photoactivation significantly improve catalytic performance for fuel cells.

items.title: The 2D approximation quickly breaks down in reflection ptychography
items.summary: Develops a 3D weak-scattering description to test the 2D approximation in reflection ptychography. It finds that reflection geometries impose much stricter thickness limits than transmission due to Ewald sphere rotation. The study shows that depth-dependent propagation in the forward model resolves reconstruction artifacts and enables sample thickness recovery.

items.title: The effect of Nb and O on the martensitic transformation in the Ti-Nb-O alloys
items.summary: Analyzes the effects of niobium and oxygen on phase stability and martensitic transformation in Ti-Nb-O alloys. It finds that niobium stabilizes the beta phase and governs martensite evolution. Oxygen modifies transformation pathways, suppressing omega phase at low niobium levels and inhibiting long-range transformation at higher levels due to interstitial-induced lattice distortions.

items.title: Indication of Stochastic Photothermal Dynamics around a Topological Defect in a Chiral Magnet
items.summary: Uses pump-probe Lorentz transmission electron microscopy to study photothermally induced phase transitions in the chiral magnet Co9Zn9Mn2. It finds that magnetic order recovery is delayed and blurred near edge dislocations. This indicates that topological defects enhance stochasticity in the recovery dynamics, causing the system to follow multiple stochastic relaxation paths.

items.title: Quantitative 3D Analysis of Porosity and Fractal Geometry in Electrochemically Etched Macroporous Silicon
items.summary: This study uses FIB-SEM tomography for quantitative 3D analysis of porosity in electrochemically etched macroporous silicon. Findings reveal that 2D surface-based estimates systematically underestimate true volumetric porosity due to pore anisotropy and branching. The results highlight the importance of direct 3D characterization for reliable morphological quantification and provide a framework for interpreting structural trends.

items.title: Grafted Low-Leakage Si/AlN p-n Diodes Enabled by Fluorinated AlN Interface
items.summary: This work introduces an interface engineering approach using fluorination-induced AlFx formation and SiNx passivation to suppress leakage in p-Si/n-AlN heterojunction diodes. The engineered interface reduces reverse leakage current by several orders of magnitude while maintaining forward conduction. These results establish AlFx/SiNx passivation as an effective strategy for stabilizing AlN interfaces in low-leakage power electronics.

items.title: Drug-delivery Ca-Mg silicate scaffolds encapsulated in PLGA
items.summary: The objective was to develop dual-functional bredigite scaffolds for bone regeneration and vancomycin delivery. The study found that while bare scaffolds showed burst release and low biocompatibility, encapsulating them in PLGA coatings modified the drug release kinetics and significantly improved cell viability.

items.title: Competition of carrier bioresorption and drug release kinetics of vancomycin-loaded silicate macroporous microspheres to determine cell biocompatibility
items.summary: This research examines the cytocompatibility of vancomycin-loaded bredigite, akermanite, and diopside macroporous microspheres. The findings indicate that diopside exhibited the highest cell viability, leading to the conclusion that carrier bioresorption kinetics play a more dominant role than drug delivery kinetics in determining biocompatibility.

items.title: Epitaxial MgSnN2 on 4H-SiC (0001): An Earth-Abundant Nitride for Green Optoelectronics and Photovoltaics
items.summary: This study demonstrates the epitaxial growth of MgSnN2 on 4H-SiC(0001) using magnetron sputtering for green optoelectronics. Findings reveal a wurtzite structure with high absorption coefficients and photoluminescence at ~2.4 eV. These results establish MgSnN2 as a viable, earth-abundant, and environmentally friendly alternative to III-nitrides for sustainable photovoltaic applications.

items.title: Atomic Structure of Grain Boundaries, Dislocations and Associated Strain in Templated Co-evaporated Photoactive Halide Perovskites
items.summary: Researchers used low-dose electron microscopy to investigate atomic-level defects in templated FA0.9Cs0.1PbI3-xClx perovskite films. The study identifies a preferred <001> grain orientation and characterizes the atomic structure of grain boundaries and edge dislocations. These findings reveal how specific structural defects and associated strain fields likely act as recombination centers, influencing solar cell performance.

items.title: Controlled topological dilution drives cooperative glassy dynamics in artificial spin ice
items.summary: Investigates how random decimation affects magnetic dynamics in artificial square spin ice. It finds that increasing topological disorder drives the system from long-range order to a glassy state. This state exhibits cooperative dynamics, aging, and a transition from thermally activated behavior to Vogel-Fulcher-type freezing, establishing the system as a tunable platform for studying glassiness.

items.title: Temperature Dependent Characteristics of Quasi-vertical AlN Schottky Diodes on Bulk AlN Substrate
items.summary: Characterizes temperature-dependent performance of quasi-vertical AlN Schottky barrier diodes on bulk AlN substrates. The devices show stable operation up to 300°C with high on/off ratios. Findings reveal that Poole-Frenkel emission dominates leakage, and an interfacial AlNxOy layer influences transport, providing critical insights for developing high-performance AlN power electronics.

items.title: Understanding ultrafast x-ray 'echoes' diffracted from single crystals
items.summary: This study uses tele-ptychography to image ultrafast x-ray diffraction echoes in perfect crystals. Researchers observed ten echo maxima with a total temporal delay under 108 fs, suggesting applications for ultrafast beam splitters and monitoring rapid structural changes in single crystals.

items.title: Bond-density-wave orders induced by geometric frustration in the kagome metal CeRu3Si2
items.summary: Researchers investigated geometric frustration in the kagome metal CeRu3Si2. They discovered bond-density-wave orders above room temperature, where interlayer bonds modulate to satisfy a zero-sum constraint. This finding establishes chemical bonding as a distinct pathway for frustration physics in quantum materials, expanding beyond traditional spin and charge systems.

items.title: Laser-generated CuPdAgPtAu High-Entropy Alloy Nanoparticles -- Thermal Segregation Threshold and Elemental Segregation
items.summary: This study investigates the elemental segregation and thermal stability of laser-generated CuPdAgPtAu high-entropy alloy nanoparticles. Findings show that rapid quenching suppresses thermodynamic segregation, while post-synthesis heating triggers Cu-Ag phase segregation, demonstrating that these nanoparticles can be stabilized under kinetic control for high-temperature catalytic applications.

items.title: Revealing buried ferroelectric topologies by depth-resolved electron diffraction imaging
items.summary: The authors developed Depth-Resolved Electron Diffraction Imaging (DREDI) to map buried ferroelectric topologies in BiFeO3 films. DREDI revealed that surface stripes evolve into subsurface flux-closure vortices and vertices, enabling volumetric studies of buried configurations driven by strain heterogeneity and ferroelastic twinning.

items.title: AI-assisted Human-in-the-Loop Web Platform for Structural Characterization in Hard drive design
items.summary: This paper presents a human-AI-assisted web platform for automated structural characterization in hard drive design. The workflow integrates peak detection with interactive correction to enable precise, scalable quantification of layer thickness and interface roughness in STEM images, bridging human insight and machine precision for semiconductor metrology.

items.title: Spatially modulated morphotropic phase boundaries in a compressively strained multiferroic thin film
items.summary: This study explores self-organizing structures of morphotropic phase boundaries in compressively strained BiFeO3 thin films. Researchers identified flat and zig-zag interphase boundaries with atomic-scale polarization rotation, suggesting these modulated structures can be engineered for enhanced piezoelectric and magnetic properties in potential device applications.

items.title: Molecular beam epitaxy of wafer-scale O-band InAs/InGaAs quantum dots on GaAs for quantum photonics
items.summary: This research develops a scalable molecular beam epitaxy strategy to produce low-density O-band InAs/InGaAs quantum dots on GaAs. By utilizing gradient deposition, the team achieved controlled dot density and demonstrated electrically tunable single-photon emission with high purity, confirming the method's potential for large-scale quantum photonic applications.

items.title: Work-Function-Resolved Imaging of Relaxation Oscillations and Chemical Spillover in CO Oxidation over Platinum Surfaces
items.summary: This study investigates local electronic mechanisms in CO oxidation on platinum using operando SEM and FM-KPFM. It provides the first work-function-resolved imaging of reaction fronts, enabling physical assignment of CO- and oxygen-covered states. Findings reveal pronounced temporal asymmetry and spatial heterogeneity in chemical wave propagation, with rapid oxygen onset followed by gradual relaxation to the CO-covered state.

items.title: Grain boundary defects induced Tc increment in MnSi
items.summary: This work investigates the microstructure and magnetic properties of MnSi thin films synthesized using magnetron sputtering and laser annealing. The study demonstrates that the Curie temperature can be increased fourfold, from 30 K to 120 K, by precisely controlling the crystal size to approximately 20 nm. This highlights laser annealing as a versatile method for fabricating miniaturized magnetic devices.

items.title: Quantification of magnetic interactions in van der Waals heterostructures using Lorentz transmission electron microscopy and electron holography
items.summary: The study quantifies magnetic interactions in Fe3GeTe2/graphite/Fe3GeTe2 heterostructures using cross-sectional Lorentz transmission electron microscopy and electron holography. It identifies a dipolar coupling length scale of 34 nm, where domain misalignment emerges and the interlayer field reduces by 50%. Results also reveal magnetic moment canting near surfaces and narrow domain walls, providing guidance for designing controllable van der Waals magnetic devices.

items.title: Designing dislocation-driven polar vortex networks in twisted perovskites
items.summary: This research explores the coupling between interfacial dislocation networks and topological vortices in twisted freestanding SrTiO3 layers. Using 4D-STEM and phase-field modeling, the authors demonstrate that interfacial reconstruction into screw dislocation networks, rather than geometric moire patterns, stabilizes long-range ordered polar vortex-antivortex arrays. This establishes twist-controlled dislocations as a method for designing local polar and electronic structures in oxides.

items.title: Channeling-in channeling-out revisited: selected area electron channeling and electron backscatter diffraction
